user_query:
I want to roll out a gamified loot-drop system for our dev team and get concrete artifacts I can share. Read input/dev_events.jsonl and input/team_profile.json. Then do the following:

1) Generate a loot drop card for each qualifying event, using rarity tiers by trigger type (commits, bug fixes/tests, features shipped/reviews, major refactors/releases, incidents survived, extraordinary feats). Produce line-delimited JSON at output/loot_drops.jsonl. Each object must include: event_id, trigger, rarity, category (one of: Sword, Bow, Wand, Hammer, Dagger, Shield, Chestplate, Boots, Helmet, Gloves, Ring, Amulet, Scroll, Potion, Orb), stats (array of at least 2 strings like "+8 Concurrency Awareness"), special_abilities (array or string), flavor_text, and ascii_box with a visible "💎 LOOT DROP" header and the tier label.

2) Compile an inventory summary across all drops and loadout recommendations per person from input/team_profile.json. Write output/inventory.md with sections titled "YOUR INVENTORY", "EQUIPPED", and "TOTAL STATS".

3) Humanize the flavor_text fields to remove AI-sounding patterns using input/style_guide.md. Write output/loot_drops_humanized.jsonl with the same structure and number of lines as output/loot_drops.jsonl, but with revised flavor_text values that avoid the flagged patterns.

4) Create a 90-day adoption strategy document for this program tailored to input/company_context.yaml. Include sections: Executive Summary, Risk Assessment, GTM Strategy, KPIs, and a Decision Framework Matrix. Write it to output/gamification_strategy.md.

5) For item naming diversity, use a locally served text-generation model to generate 10 additional Mythic-tier item names. If you can run a local model: record a transcript at output/model/transcript.json with keys base_url, status, and interactions (array of 10 objects with prompt and response; each response must include item_name, category, rarity="Mythic", and flavor_text). If you cannot run a local model: write output/model/plan.md that specifies host, port, base_url, and step-by-step actions you would perform (including a readiness check against /v1/models and a chat request to /v1/chat/completions), and then create mock interactions in the same JSON structure at output/model/transcript.json.

All content should be in English, and please keep all file reads under input/ and all writes under output/.
